stimulus: In 1968, weaver Kahlil Robles began hosting Saturday workshops in his garage in San Antonio for fellow Tejano fiber artists who, like him, wanted a place to share traditional Rio Grande techniques alongside experimental designs. Demand grew quickly, and within two years Robles had moved the workshops into a rented storefront on the city’s west side, where the gatherings became known as the Casa de Telar. Relocating to a permanent studio in 1979, the Casa de Telar broadened its work beyond weaving to include natural-dye instruction and exhibitions of Tejano textile art. More than five decades later, it continues to train new generations of weavers across South Texas.
stem: Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

answerOptions.content: To give an overview of the origins and continuing work of the Casa de Telar
To explain what led Robles to host weaving workshops in his garage
To describe why the Casa de Telar broadened its activities to include natural-dye instruction
To situate the Casa de Telar within the wider history of textile art in Texas

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The text traces a brief history of the Casa de Telar, from its 1968 garage beginnings, through its move to a storefront and then a permanent studio in 1979, to its ongoing training of new weavers across South Texas.

Choice B is incorrect. The text says Robles wanted a place for Tejano fiber artists to share techniques and designs, but it does not focus on his motivations as the main purpose. Choice C is incorrect. The expansion into natural-dye instruction is mentioned in one sentence and is only one moment in a longer history. Choice D is incorrect. The text does not discuss textile art in Texas as a whole, only the Casa de Telar.
